title: The Forest That Refused to Split

features.name: the root-net
features.desc-on-touch: a web of roots visible beneath the surface; every tree touches every other tree through the dark below, sharing what one tree knows

features.name: the spring-source
features.desc-on-touch: where water and growth begin, before the forest learned to divide them into separate concerns; they still remember being one thing

features.name: the canopy-circuit
features.desc-on-touch: overhead, the branches meet in an unbroken circuit; light passes through without choosing a path or a favourite tree

features.name: the undivided glyph
features.desc-on-touch: a symbol representing unity before grammar; the archive found it encoded in the growth rings of the oldest tree, a signature in wood

features.name: the breathing border
features.desc-on-touch: where the forest ends and refuses to end; it takes one more step back each season, refusing any final edge or conclusion

inscription: here the forest refused to learn that spring and root were ever separate things

file.name: Undivided_Canopy_Archive_Growth_History.txt
file.note: before the mesh learned to compare, the forest kept itself as one continuous thought across root and branch

exits.label: back toward the canopy archive that holds this memory
deeper to where the pre-split clearing dreams in both directions at once
upward to the forest before it learned what timber was
westward to where the forest's own sources begin unnamed
